steps.title: Order the events on a timeline
steps.content: Two past events are named: serving in World War II (1941–1945) and joining the New York Knicks for the 1947–48 season. The WWII service was completed before he joined the Knicks.

steps.title: Pick the tense that shows "before another past event"
steps.content: When one past action finishes before a second past action, English uses the past perfect: "had" + past participle. The word "already" in the sentence also signals a completed prior action, which lines up with past perfect.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• "already served": simple past treats the WWII service as just another event on the same level as joining the Knicks; it loses the "before that" relationship. ✗
  • "was already serving": past progressive says the service was ongoing at the time he joined the Knicks, but the war had ended two years earlier. ✗
  • "already serves": present tense conflicts with the clearly past time frame (1941–45, 1947–48). ✗
  • "had already served": past perfect places the completed WWII service before the later moment of joining the Knicks. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
